title: Verify MCP Scoring Engine v1
description: Specifies how VerifyMCP consumes criteria, evidence, and plugin results to produce a deterministic VerificationArtifact with an overall verdict.

scoring_algorithm.description: Ordered steps the engine executes for each verification request.

steps.name: validate_inputs
steps.description: Verify all required inputs are present: verification_request_id, correlation_id, execution_plan, build_report, evidence_ids.

on_missing.rationale: Fail-closed: missing inputs cannot be verified.

steps.name: extract_criteria
steps.description: Extract all acceptance_criteria from the ExecutionPlan. Each criterion has criteria_id, description, required flag, and verification_type.

steps.name: resolve_evidence
steps.description: For each criteria_id, resolve evidence_ids from BuildReport.evidence_map via the evidence registry. Verify evidence exists and is accessible.

on_missing_evidence.rationale: FC-001: required criterion without corresponding evidence.

steps.name: select_plugins
steps.description: For each criterion, select the appropriate verifier plugin based on verification_type (unit, integration, e2e, visual, api, freshness).

on_no_plugin.rationale: No plugin available for required verification type.

steps.name: execute_plugins
steps.description: Execute each selected plugin with the criterion's evidence. Plugin runs in sandbox with bounded timeout and resource limits per the plugin ABI.

on_plugin_error.rationale: Plugin execution failure. Fail-closed: cannot verify.

on_plugin_timeout.rationale: Plugin exceeded timeout. Fail-closed: cannot verify.

steps.name: evaluate_criteria
steps.description: Map each plugin result to a criterion status (pass|warn|fail|blocked). Apply fail-closed rules.
rules: Plugin result pass -> criteria status pass
Plugin result warn + required criterion -> criteria status blocked (FC-002)
Plugin result warn + optional criterion -> criteria status warn (rationale required)
Plugin result fail -> criteria status fail
Plugin result error/timeout -> criteria status blocked

steps.name: check_ladder_compliance
steps.description: Verify that all check types in the workflow class ladder have been executed and passed.

on_incomplete_ladder.rationale: Ladder compliance failure: required check type not executed or not passed.

steps.name: check_freshness
steps.description: If freshness_required, execute freshness checks on time-sensitive claims from ResearchReport or ExecutionPlan.

on_stale_claim.rationale: FC-005: freshness check failure on time-sensitive claim.

steps.name: compute_overall_status
steps.description: Aggregate individual criteria statuses into overall_status using the aggregation rules.

steps.name: produce_artifact
steps.description: Produce the VerificationArtifact with all criteria_results, freshness_results, ladder_compliance, and overall_status. Compute artifact_hash.

overall_status_aggregation.description: Rules for computing overall_status from individual criteria statuses.

rules.condition: Any required criterion has status blocked
rules.rationale: FC-001/FC-003: blocked evidence cannot produce pass.

rules.condition: Any required criterion has status fail
rules.rationale: FC-003: failed required criterion prevents pass.

rules.condition: Any required criterion has status warn (without policy exception)
rules.rationale: FC-002: required warn treated as blocked.

rules.condition: All required criteria are pass, some optional criteria are warn
rules.rationale: Optional warn with rationale allows continuation.

overall_status_aggregation.rule: Overall status is the highest-severity status among required criteria. OSA rules evaluated in order; first match wins.

fail_closed_enforcement.description: How FC-001 through FC-005 are enforced in the engine.

implementations.mechanism: Missing evidence for required criterion -> blocked
Required criterion with warn -> blocked unless policy exception
Any required non-pass -> overall cannot be pass
fail_closed_enforced is always set to true. Artifact rejected if false.
Stale time-sensitive claim -> fail, not warn

authority_conformance_enforcement.description: How AC-001 through AC-005 are enforced in the engine.

implementations.mechanism: Every schema field must have fieldOwnership entry. Missing -> blocked.
Every fieldOwnership path must resolve. Orphaned -> blocked.
Planner-writtenBy fields checked for routing content. Violation -> fail.
Orchestrator-writtenBy fields checked for plan content. Violation -> fail.
BuildReport.evidence_map must cover all criteria_ids. Missing -> blocked.

artifact_hash_computation.scope: All fields of the VerificationArtifact except artifact_hash itself.
artifact_hash_computation.immutability_rule: Once artifact_hash is computed and the artifact is persisted, no field may be modified.

engine_invariants.rule: The engine always produces exactly one VerificationArtifact per verification request.
The engine never produces overall_status: pass if any required criterion is not pass.
The engine is deterministic: same inputs produce the same artifact (excluding timestamps).
Plugin failures are fail-closed: error or timeout -> blocked, never silently skipped.
correlation_id in the artifact must be byte-identical to the request correlation_id.
